description_long: This sculpture features three life-sized, nude female figures standing in a circular arrangement atop a hexagonal stone platform. Each figure is rendered in a classical style, with detailed anatomical features and short, stylized hair. The women appear to be interacting, with two facing forward and one with her back to the viewer, their hands gently gesturing toward each other. The figures wear what appear to be flat, shallow vessels or headdresses on their heads, adding a unique element to the composition. The entire group is mounted on a large, rectangular stone pedestal, which includes a small plaque on one side. The surface of the sculpture is a weathered gray, suggesting it is made of stone or concrete, and the base sits on a patch of grass or earth.
description: A classical-style sculpture of three nude female figures standing in a circle atop a stone pedestal, each with a shallow vessel on her head. The figures are detailed and lifelike, with a weathered gray finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing two female figures facing forward and one with her back to the viewer, all standing on a hexagonal platform atop a stone pedestal with a visible plaque.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the figures and the circular arrangement, with the figures' arms and vessels on their heads clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of two figures and the front of the third, emphasizing the anatomical detail and the arrangement of the group.
description_view_3: Angled side view showing the interaction between the figures and the depth of the composition, with the stone base and ground visible.
attribute_subject_matter: three nude female figures in a classical style
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is installed outdoors, likely in a park or public urban space, as indicated by the stone pedestal and surrounding grass.
